mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive forward returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer demand and construction activity, which expands corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative mean forward return or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side momentum that boosts short-term earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns during tightening periods or the 5-day forward return correlation with INDPRO changes falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment capacity that supports near-term corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with payroll growth or the rolling correlation drops below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, the elevated cost of capital suppresses corporate investment and equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of periods above the threshold or the signal loses predictive power in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansions in industrial production and real GDP signal broad-based economic output growth that strengthens earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields a negative mean return or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales reflects robust consumer demand that directly translates into higher corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative returns during expansion periods or the out-of-sample hit rate falls below 50%.
